title: periploca somaliensis
intro: Periploca somaliensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the Somali milkvine. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or ointments, and it is also used in some African cultures as a remedy for snake bites and other poisons.
